header.stance: 油價回落支撐美債曲線腹部，實質殖利率則壓抑長端

zh-TW.long_thesis_markdown:
## 固定收益開篇論點

5月29日美國公債收盤走勢中斷了前一交易日由避險需求帶動的存續期間漲勢，但未扭轉本週較廣泛的復甦。短端與中期債券維持小幅買盤，10年期收平，長債則略為走弱，使殖利率曲線趨陡。主要解讀是，油價下跌消除部分眼前的通膨補償與升息風險，但面對具韌性的美國經濟活動、尚未明朗的地緣政治結果、全球央行收緊政策，以及龐大的主權債券存續期間供給，投資人仍保留可觀的實質利率與期限溢價緩衝。這是對通膨衝擊持續性的重新定價，而非重返寬鬆週期。

油價相關利多是盤中最強的傳導管道。市場期待美國與伊朗達成協議，曾將美國公債殖利率拉向本週低點；但總統會議結束而未作出決定後，部分走勢回吐。4月貿易與庫存數據亦不支持單純的經濟放緩敘事：商品貿易逆差收窄，但出口與進口均上升；批發庫存累積放緩，零售庫存則高於預期。異常大量的新發票據與債券納入基準指數後，月底指數需求在收盤前支撐附息債券，但這股機械性買盤仍未阻止30年期殖利率收高。

政策的不對稱性仍是主要反作用力。堪薩斯城聯準銀行總裁Jeff Schmid強調通膨問題，以及保留利率雙向調整選項的必要性。Michelle Bowman主張，只要公信力維持穩固，便可忽略暫時性的能源通膨；Mary Daly則認為沒有迫切需要改變現行政策設定。因此，原油下跌雖緩和眼前的緊縮壓力，但Fed內部觀點的分歧仍使短端保有升息選擇權。信用總報酬指標溫和上漲、股票走強及波動率下降，顯示美國公債買盤並非源於避險退場。

海外發展進一步強化長端所受的壓抑。歐元區各國通膨數據增強ECB升息的理由；日本2年期公債標售結果略顯疲弱，則顯示儘管東京通膨降溫，近期BOJ政策正常化仍會影響需求。加拿大經濟成長疲弱確實形成抵銷力量，但不足以主導全球存續期間走勢。最終收盤表現已不同於前一日由經濟成長疑慮帶動的實質殖利率回落：10年期通膨補償下降，實質殖利率與最長端則停止上漲。相較於政策不確定性、財政供給或長期資本稀缺的定價，市場更願意下調油價衝擊長期持續的機率。

## 美債利率、Fed定價、實質殖利率與殖利率曲線結構

各天期表現呈現殖利率曲線腹部溫和上漲、最長端賣壓的格局。2年期殖利率收於3.98%，下降1個基點；5年期收於4.13%，下降2個基點。10年期維持在4.45%，30年期則上升1個基點至4.99%。2s10s殖利率曲線趨陡1個基點至47個基點，2s30s趨陡2個基點至101。5年期表現領先，使2s5s10s蝶式利差下降3個基點至-17，2s10s30s蝶式利差則維持在-7。此一形狀顯示，利多集中於最易受近期通膨衝擊減弱影響的區段，同時保留長端溢價。

通膨連結債券的拆解進一步凸顯此一訊息。10年期實質殖利率上升1個基點至2.07%，10年期損益兩平通膨率下降1個基點至2.38%，使名目10年期殖利率維持不變。5年期與7年期實質殖利率分別穩定在1.61%及1.83%，即使名目5年期債券上漲亦然。較長天期方面，20年期與30年期實質殖利率各上升1個基點，至2.49%及2.71%。因此，油價回落的效果反映在通膨補償上，但投資人並未降低對長存續期間政府債券所要求的實質報酬。

單日的遲疑出現在整週更強勁的行情之中。2年、5年、10年及30年期殖利率較前一個週五分別下降15、14、11及8個基點。隨著基準油價因市場預期中東供給可能恢復正常而跌至6週低點，美國公債漲勢收復了部分戰爭引發的跌幅。週五，Donald Trump總統表示關於伊朗的會議正在進行後，2年至10年期殖利率觸及本週低點。隨後傳出他離場時未作出最終決定，價格因而回吐部分漲幅。30年期殖利率盤中觸及4.96%，但收盤較該低點高出3個基點，顯示地緣政治利多多快便遭遇長端阻力。

美國公布的數據未提供足以迫使市場出現更大走勢的單一經濟成長結論。4月商品貿易逆差初值由修正後的$853億收窄至$824億，優於$865億的市場共識。出口增加$85億至$2,197億，進口則增加$56億至$3,021億。淨出口的計算結果改善，但雙向貿易均增強，因此並不代表國內需求崩跌。批發庫存增加0.5%，低於0.8%的市場共識，也遠低於3月修正後的1.5%增幅。零售庫存增加0.7%，高於0.4%的市場共識，其中不含汽車的庫存增加0.6%，汽車經銷商庫存增加0.9%。庫存累積支撐近期GDP核算，但最終需求仍不明朗。

Fed的溝通在經濟活動數據好壞參半的背景下保留政策選擇空間。Schmid表示，4月PCE物價較1年前上升3.8%後，通膨仍然過熱；隨著數據演變，官員應保留上調或下調政策利率的彈性。他也將勞動市場描述為平衡，並主張勞動力成長下降可以與招聘放緩及失業率穩定並存。即使就業市場並未過熱，他的論述仍將通膨帶動的升息保留在政策反應函數之中。

Bowman則提供內部的制衡觀點。她主張，若能源引發的通膨衝擊只是暫時現象，便應予以忽略，因為進一步收緊政策可能不必要地削弱經濟活動與就業。持續性是她的界線：衝突延長、油價持續高企，或更廣泛地傳導至PCE通膨，都可能改變她對風險的權衡。Daly另表示沒有迫切需要調整利率，費城聯準銀行總裁Anna Paulson則認為，市場經歷的是一連串價格衝擊，尚無證據顯示通膨結構已改變。交換市場仍反映年底前約15個基點的緊縮幅度，足以抑制2年期債券漲勢，即使原油下跌已降低立即採取行動的理由。

技術性需求放大附息債券買盤，但未改變政策結論。當月發行的約$3,240億票據與債券，於紐約時間下午4時納入1項主要美國公債指數，促使被動型基金再平衡，期貨成交量異常龐大。此次新增規模高於3月底與4月底納入的約$2,400億。價格在再平衡時段上漲，但仍低於盤中高點。這有助解釋為何在總體數據好壞參半之際，曲線腹部收盤仍較為強勁，也說明該收盤走勢並不代表市場獨立判定通膨衝擊或升息尾端風險已經消失。

下午5時截止時間後不久公布的1項策略修正，凸顯殘存的存續期間疑慮，但並不能解釋已完成的收盤走勢。Deutsche Bank將其預估的12月10年期殖利率高點，由先前接近當時4.45%水準的年底預測，上調至4.70%，理由是Fed已結束降息，並將維持在長期中性政策設定。該行將這種按兵不動情境，與市場定價所反映年底前約55%的升息機率加以區別。2種路徑都使持續漲勢的障礙繼續存在：油價下跌可以降低通膨補償，但實質利率若要持久回落，仍需經濟活動轉弱或政策方向更加明確。

## 信用、融資與初級市場承接

信用市場以溫和正總報酬作收，短天期融資並未出現明顯斷裂。LQD上漲0.09%、HYG上漲0.10%、JNK上漲0.16%，在股票走強且美國公債殖利率曲線略為趨陡之際，漲幅仍相對克制。等值息票國庫券殖利率介於3.68%至3.79%；4週至52週各天期的單日變動均未超過1個基點。整體格局偏正面，但更具資訊價值的訊號來自新交易定價：凡結構、稀缺性或利差提供足夠補償之處，投資人皆願意提供資本，但對存續期間與槓桿仍十分敏感。

日本銀行資本工具提供最明確的已完成承接案例。Sumitomo Mitsui Financial Group完成1筆¥3,000億的雙券種其他第1類資本交易定價，吸引約¥3,900億訂單，主要來自壽險公司與信託銀行。2券種定價均較Mitsubishi UFJ於4月發行的可比AT1證券收窄2個基點，贖回結構分別設於5.25年及10.25年後。這些證券在資本結構中的順位偏低，若資本比率跌破觸發門檻，可能轉換為股權；因此，訂單反映的是對獲得補償之次順位風險的需求，而非一般性的銀行高順位債曝險。

這筆交易也傳達供給與時機訊息。在計入SMFG發行案之前，日本AT1發行量已較1年前增加逾4倍至¥7,100億，因銀行在首次贖回日前進行再融資，並為企業放款補充資本。市場預期BOJ利率走高，促使發行人提早發債，以免重設利率及基準成本進一步上升。債券贖回創造替代需求，次順位債供給有限亦有助市場承接交易。市場准入能力強勁，同時發行人也積極趕在國內利率環境轉差前鎖定融資。

Credit Agricole的¥1,065億、7券種武士債發行案顯示，同一批投資人仍要求差異化定價。其5年期A+票據以高於TONA中間交換利率58個基點定價，票面利率為2.454%；利差是Sumitomo Mitsui Banking Corp.於4月發行、評等較低之A級票據28個基點利差的2倍。截至5月28日，海外發行人在武士債市場已發行¥6,627億，創7年來同期最繁忙紀錄。日圓殖利率上升提高絕對收益，但投資人不願延長天期，使利差較寬的中期債券比長存續期間債券更具吸引力。

這2筆日本交易顯示，市場承接能力仍有界線。監管資本工具的稀缺性使SMFG得以較近期可比證券收窄定價；Credit Agricole則儘管評等較高，仍支付顯著的跨發行人溢價。無風險殖利率上升並未使市場關閉，而是改變買方對票息、利差、贖回風險與天期的要求。這項差異也說明，為何美國信用基金小幅上漲可與龐大的全球發債日程並存。融資能力依然可用，但定價權取決於資本結構順位與存續期間負擔。

週末報導增添了1項更大規模的前瞻考驗。由JPMorgan領導的銀行團正以長期債務取代$150億的Warner Bros.過橋貸款，並於本週兩度擴大定期貸款B的規模。該貸款取得超過$300億訂單，受惠於約BB級的信用輪廓及槓桿貸款供給稀缺。規模更廣泛的Paramount Skydance融資案，暫定分為約$300億投資等級債券、$120億高收益票據及$75億貸款，出售時程仍未確定。Paramount為爭取投資等級評等，公開取得Ellison家族承諾，若有必要將注入資本，以使槓桿維持符合預測。

截至觀察期間結束，該融資案尚未證明市場能承接完整的收購融資。然而，其結構已揭示市場下一個潛在斷層。浮動利率貸款需求強勁，足以擴大過橋貸款再融資；美國垃圾債發行量則較2025年同期高出逾35%。高收益債券部分仍需投資人為大量集中的媒體業槓桿定價，投資等級部分則取決於評等處理及贊助方支持。因此，整體信用市場的良性收盤掩蓋了1項即將到來的配置決策，牽涉不同評等級距與融資工具。

## 全球存續期間、央行與財政傳導

歐洲通膨是同日對美國存續期間最強的全球性制約。5月整體通膨率在法國加速至2.8%、義大利3.3%、西班牙3.6%。德國放緩至2.7%，但部分降溫來自暫時性燃油稅減免與食品價格下降，基礎價格壓力及企業定價意向仍較為強勁。市場維持ECB升息25個基點並於年內再次升息的預期。此一格局抬高全球短端利率下限，限制美國長端將油價下跌視為政策全面鬆綁的空間。

ECB官員的評論將公信力置於預設的政策步調之前。義大利央行總裁Fabio Panetta表示，衝突持續、燃料庫存耗減及通膨預期上升，足以支持重新校準政策；但薪資尚未呈現螺旋式上升，中期預期亦保持穩定。Bulgarian央行總裁Dimitar Radev主張，若預期鬆動，延遲行動的代價可能更高。經濟成長的制衡力量可見於法國需求轉弱，以及Panetta對義大利經濟活動可能停滯的警告；義大利公共債務高於GDP的130%，亦使其殖利率曲線暴露於融資成本上升的風險。升息1次或可處理預期脫錨風險；是否形成更長的升息週期，仍取決於價格傳導與經濟成長。

日本呈現另一種張力：實際通膨降溫，政策正常化預期卻保持堅挺。東京不含生鮮食品的CPI年增率放緩至1.3%，為4年來最低；不含生鮮食品與能源的指標則上升1.6%。水費、托育與汽油補助壓低整體數據，服務價格卻上升1.1%，不含生鮮品的食品價格增加4.1%。隔夜指數交換反映近期BOJ升息機率約為77%。日圓接近每美元159，加上已宣布的食品漲價，使輸入型及企業價格傳導風險在整體數據放緩之際依然存在。

2年期JGB標售將政策張力轉化為需求表現。投標倍數由前次標售的5.24降至3.7，接近3.74的12個月平均值；標售尾差則為12月以來最大。標售順利完成，但在殖利率本週已下降後，買方並不急於進場。投資人一方面衡量處於數十年高點的短期殖利率，另一方面評估BOJ再次升息及減少購債的風險。對美國公債而言，日本短端漲勢若可靠度降低，加上日本國內殖利率更具吸引力，都會提高跨境需求的門檻，並有助於維持全球期限溢價。

加拿大提供已開發市場最明確的經濟成長抵銷力量。第1季支出面GDP大致持平，相當於年化萎縮0.1%，遠低於1.5%的市場共識；第4季則修正為萎縮1.0%。住宅投資以年化7.9%的速度下降。3月按產業別計算的產出減少0.1%，其中商品生產萎縮0.8%，抵銷服務業0.1%的成長；不過，4月初步估值顯示可能反彈0.4%。上述結果透過經濟成長管道有利加拿大債券存續期間，但第2季初步數據改善，仍無法支持單向的衰退結論。

澳洲則呈現相反的結構性方向。預估超過A$1,550億、相當於年度產出的5.6%的資料中心投資計畫，被估計可支撐約400,000個工作機會；另有約A$2,000億的潔淨能源專案。資本深化的論點支持較高的中性利率，即使最終生產力提升能改善供給。澳洲央行現金利率在年內3次升息後已達4.35%，投資熱潮再添全球實質殖利率維持在疫情前常態以上的理由。這是資本需求管道，不同於當日油價帶動的通膨壓力緩解。

紐約收盤後的發展強化全球市場後續訊號，但並不屬於週五價格形成的1部分。中國官方製造業PMI於週日下滑至50.0；儘管生產指數為51.2，新訂單卻降至49.9，非製造業指數亦僅升至50.1。此一組合削弱全球需求動能，同時暴露訂單動能疲弱。英格蘭銀行政策官員Catherine Mann週六警告，過去的穩定時期已被更易受衝擊的通膨時代取代；市場則預期英國年底前升息25個基點。週日，ECB委員Alvaro Santos Pereira支持加快行動，Luis de Guindos則描述1種先衝擊通膨、再打擊經濟成長的供給衝擊。這些較晚出現的訊號深化了下一交易日將面對的政策與成長衝突。

## 新興市場主權債、美元流動性與債務管理風險

新興市場債券參與了平靜的收盤行情，但未形成一致的主權國家訊號。美元走弱且兩大原油基準均下跌，使EMB上漲0.21%，為廣泛信用基金中單日漲幅最高者。此一組合緩解了硬通貨融資及能源進口帳單的即時壓力。黃金同步上漲且美元波動幅度有限，意味不宜將本交易日解讀為地緣政治風險全面消退。各國敏感度仍取決於進口依賴程度、貨幣公信力，以及國內央行承受通膨衝擊的意願。

印度在美國截止時間後，成為最具體的主權政策案例。財政部於週六警告，原油價格高企、盧比貶值、上游成本上升，以及季風偏弱的風險，都需要持續警戒。4月批發通膨率由3.88%加速至8.30%，高於5.50%的市場共識，儘管零售通膨仍低於印度央行的4%目標。盧比本月曾交易於接近每美元97的歷史低點。對本地利率與外部融資而言，傳導路徑從荷姆茲海峽，經由進口帳單及生產者成本，進一步傳至消費者物價、匯率防衛，以及更為受限的成長政策緩衝空間。

中國週末PMI為新興市場增添另一項制約。生產指數仍高於擴張分界線，但新訂單低於50，製造業整體指數亦下降，顯示外部需求動能轉弱。大宗商品進口國可能受惠於油價下降，出口國則仍面臨訂單管道的不確定性。對存續期間而言，此一組合比對信用成長更加有利，尤其是財政或準主權借款人依賴貿易現金流的市場。這也使EMB收盤上漲的正面訊號更為複雜：美國殖利率下降與美元走弱有利硬通貨報酬，但中國需求放緩可能削弱個別發行人的基本面展望。

Fed理事Christopher Waller於週日說明1項較長期的美元流動性機制。美元穩定幣發行人通常持有美元或美國國庫券等流動性擔保資產；Waller主張，採用穩定幣的國家將以類似固定匯率的方式輸入美國貨幣條件。此一管道仍屬前瞻性質：更廣泛的採用可能擴大對短期美國公債資產的需求，並使Fed政策設定更直接地傳導至海外金融條件。這不會消除主權借款人的準備資產、監管或貨幣錯配風險；即使各國國內週期需要不同政策立場，它仍會延伸美國政策的影響範圍。

## 跨資產

風險資產強化了美國公債走勢源於通膨壓力緩解，而非防禦性需求的看法。S&P 500上漲0.22%，Nasdaq 100上漲0.36%；半導體指數在本週大漲後則大致持平。VIX下跌2.67%至15.32。股票市場可以承受短端與曲線腹部的小幅漲勢，並未將好壞參半的貿易與庫存數據視為衰退警訊。波動率下降也與穩定的國庫券殖利率曲線及信用市場溫和正報酬一致，沒有跡象顯示去槓桿被迫帶動美國公債買盤。

大宗商品傳達了最明確的因果訊號。投資人預期中東供給恢復正常可望取得進展，WTI下跌1.73%至$87.36，Brent下跌1.77%至$92.05。這些走勢降低短期與中期通膨定價中隱含的邊際能源傳導，但原油價格仍高到足以使Fed及海外官員保留緊縮選擇空間。美國會議結束而未作出決定後，美國公債盤中漲勢消退，顯示地緣政治折價仍有條件，尚未塵埃落定。

美元指數下跌0.11%至98.91，略為放鬆全球美元環境，並與新興市場債券指標走強相互呼應。黃金則反向上漲1.36%至$4,560.50，即使股票走強且波動率下降亦然。金價展現韌性，顯示市場仍有對地緣政治、通膨及政策不確定性的保障需求，儘管油價已下跌。綜合而言，跨資產表現支持範圍有限的解讀：能源長期短缺的機率下降，使風險資產與美國公債殖利率曲線腹部改善；但避險需求及長端實質殖利率上升，使整體通膨與財政體制仍未定案。

## 前瞻市場意涵

美國經濟活動與勞動數據序列，將由6月1日美東時間10:00公布的5月製造業ISM揭開序幕；隨後依序為6月2日的4月JOLTS、6月3日的ADP與服務業ISM、6月4日的初領失業救濟金人數，以及6月5日美東時間08:30的就業報告。決定性的型態是新訂單、招聘廣度與支付價格三者的組合。若訂單與就業轉弱，價格組成指標同步降溫，之後非農就業成長低於FHLB 紐約調查預估的95,000，或失業率高於4.3%，便可確認本週殖利率下降能透過實質利率回落而延續，並使2年至5年期區段承受最大的重新定價壓力。若需求具韌性、勞動市場流動穩定且價格堅挺，將挑戰油價回落的論點，保留Schmid的緊縮選擇空間，並使殖利率曲線同時面臨短端殖利率上升及損益兩平通膨率再度走高的風險。

6月2日美東時間05:00公布的歐元區HICP初值，是ECB於6月10日至11日決策前，檢驗各國能源通膨是否正在擴散的最明確指標。若整體與核心通膨率高於Wells Fargo預測的3.3%及2.5%，尤其服務或非能源商品價格加速，將證實政策需要的不只是1次重新校準，並維持歐洲殖利率及美國長天期公債所隱含全球期限溢價的上行壓力。若漲勢集中於能源，薪資維持穩定且基礎價格壓力轉弱，則將支持Panetta逐次會議決策的審慎立場。6月5日美東時間00:30的印度央行決策與6月15日至16日的BOJ會議，將同一考驗延伸至對進口敏感的亞洲：貨幣走弱、價格傳導擴大，或BOJ在目前市場約77%的機率定價下升息，都將強化跨境緊縮；相反地，匯率穩定且基礎通膨受控，則會挑戰海外政策必須使美國存續期間維持昂貴的看法。

美國財政部預定的買回作業及Paramount融資案，將顯示穩定融資能否同時承接主權與企業供給。6月3日的流動性支援操作，可能接受最多$20億的20年至30年期名目附息債券，於6月4日交割；6月4日的現金管理操作，可能接受最多$125億的1個月至2年期附息債券，於6月5日交割。報價、交割與國庫券市場交易若有序進行，將確認即使殖利率曲線趨陡，月底漲勢仍受到運作正常的中介功能支撐。企業端對應的考驗，是仍未排定時程、暫定涵蓋投資等級債券、高收益票據及貸款的融資案。若定價修正有限、訂單簿需求廣泛，且擬議的評等分級得以維持，將證明只要結構與贊助方支持具公信力，需求便能消化槓桿。若出現大幅讓利、債券部分縮減或交易商曝險增加，則將挑戰信用市場的良性收盤，並重新引發存續期間與收購融資供給是否已開始競逐相同資產負債表承接能力的疑問。
zh-TW.methodology_note: 除非另有說明，市場水準均以紐約收盤為基準。

overall_summary.headline: 油價回落帶動美債曲線腹部上漲，實質殖利率牽制長端
overall_summary.session_narrative: 美國公債收盤時，殖利率曲線腹部小幅上漲，10年期區段持平，長債則略為走弱。與油價相關的地緣政治樂觀情緒緩和近期通膨衝擊，月底指數需求亦支撐附息債券。然而，地緣政治結果仍未明朗、經濟數據好壞參半而非明顯疲弱，加上全球政策與供給風險維持長端實質殖利率堅挺，漲勢因而自盤中高點回落。
overall_summary.summary: 5月29日收盤時，短端與中期美國公債維持溫和買盤，長債則小幅走弱，使殖利率曲線趨陡。油價下跌壓低通膨補償，但具韌性的經濟活動、Fed雙向政策選擇空間，以及全球存續期間供給，仍使實質利率與期限溢價面臨阻力。

section_summaries.market_read: 收盤走勢有利中期債券的舒緩行情，同時長端的體制性溢價依然存在。
section_summaries.next_check: 經濟活動轉弱能否壓低實質殖利率，是檢驗漲勢持久性的關鍵。
section_summaries.summary: 油價下跌緩解近期通膨壓力並支撐短端與曲線腹部，但5月29日美國公債收盤時殖利率曲線趨陡，因實質利率、政策與供給溢價壓抑長端。月底需求有利附息債券，卻未使地緣政治不確定性或具韌性的經濟活動轉化為明確的寬鬆訊號。

section_summaries.market_read: 曲線腹部走強反映通膨壓力緩解，而非政策或期限溢價全面消退。
section_summaries.next_check: 觀察經濟活動轉弱是否會拉低實質殖利率，而非僅壓低損益兩平通膨率。
section_summaries.section_title: 美債利率、Fed定價、實質殖利率與殖利率曲線結構
section_summaries.summary: 殖利率曲線至5年期區段溫和上漲，長債則走弱，使油價回落的利多集中於損益兩平通膨率與曲線腹部。10年期及更長天期的實質殖利率上升，Fed官員保留雙向政策選擇空間，月底指數需求亦未能維持盤中高點。

section_summaries.market_read: 融資功能維持正常，但整體平靜掩蓋了個別證券層面的高度選擇性。
section_summaries.next_check: Paramount融資案必須展現廣泛需求，且不得出現大幅讓利或交易商部位累積。
section_summaries.section_title: 信用、融資與初級市場承接
section_summaries.summary: 信用基金小幅上漲，國庫券維持穩定；日本AT1與武士債交易則顯示，資本仍能以差異化價格取得。稀缺性與結構支撐需求，但隨著規模龐大、時程未定的Paramount融資案逼近，存續期間、槓桿與評等處理方式仍相當重要。

section_summaries.market_read: 海外政策與資本需求限制了美國長存續期間債券利多行情的延伸幅度。
section_summaries.next_check: 歐元區通膨廣度與亞洲政策傳導，是全球存續期間接下來的觀察重點。
section_summaries.section_title: 全球存續期間、央行與財政傳導
section_summaries.summary: 歐洲通膨堅挺及ECB升息預期為全球殖利率提供下檔支撐；日本通膨數據雖較疲弱，仍未消除BOJ政策正常化風險。加拿大經濟成長疲弱形成抵銷力量，但日本標售的審慎情緒與澳洲資本需求展望，仍維持全球期限溢價的上行壓力。

section_summaries.market_read: 新興市場的廣泛買盤反映外部條件轉佳，而非所有主權國家的基本面一致改善。
section_summaries.next_check: 觀察匯率穩定能否承受能源成本再度上升或全球政策進一步收緊。
section_summaries.section_title: 新興市場主權債、美元流動性與債務管理風險
section_summaries.summary: 美元走弱與油價下跌在收盤時支撐新興市場債券，緩解眼前的融資及進口壓力。黃金走強及匯率變動幅度有限，顯示地緣政治審慎情緒依然存在；各主權國家的表現則仍取決於進口曝險、貨幣公信力及國內政策空間。

section_summaries.market_read: 跨資產表現印證的是範圍有限的通膨壓力緩解，而非避險買盤帶動的漲勢。
section_summaries.next_check: 油價對地緣政治的敏感度必須進一步下降，美國公債漲勢才會擴大。
section_summaries.summary: 油價下跌降低市場對能源衝擊持續性的判斷，帶動股票走強、波動率下降，並支撐美債曲線腹部溫和買盤，但未構成防禦性訊號。美元走弱改善外部條件，但黃金走強與長端實質殖利率上升，顯示地緣政治及政策避險活動仍然活躍。

section_summaries.market_read: 下一波重新定價取決於實質利率回落能否經受數據、政策與供給的考驗。
section_summaries.next_check: 追蹤這3項考驗，以確認漲勢能否超越油價回落所帶來的利多。
section_summaries.summary: 3項考驗將決定美國公債本週的復甦能否延續：美國經濟活動與勞動數據、全球通膨與央行回應，以及主權與企業供給的承接情況。需求降溫且價格壓力減弱，將開啟實質利率回落空間；通膨持續或發債成本偏高，則會重新帶來短端與期限溢價壓力。

watch_events.market_implication: 實質利率回落將延續漲勢，其中2年至5年期區段最為敏感。
watch_events.market_trigger: 訂單與招聘轉弱、價格降溫，且非農就業低於95,000或失業率高於4.3%。
watch_events.title: 美國經濟活動與勞動數據序列
watch_events.watch_line: 追蹤整個數據序列中的訂單、招聘廣度、支付價格、非農就業及失業率。

watch_events.market_implication: 全球殖利率與美國公債期限溢價將維持高檔，限制長存續期間債券的利多。
watch_events.market_trigger: 歐元區整體與核心通膨高於3.3%及2.5%，或亞洲因傳導擴大而收緊政策。
watch_events.title: 全球通膨與政策考驗
watch_events.watch_line: 比較歐元區通膨廣度，以及印度央行與BOJ對匯率及進口壓力的回應。

watch_events.market_implication: 儘管面臨其他存續期間供給競爭，穩定的中介功能仍將支撐信用市場的良性收盤格局。
watch_events.market_trigger: 買回作業有序，企業訂單簿需求廣泛，且未出現大幅讓利或交易商部位累積。
watch_events.title: 主權與企業供給承接
watch_events.watch_line: 評估美國財政部買回交割情況，以及Paramount融資案能否在各評等級距順利完成定價。

long_thesis.english_markdown:
## Opening Fixed-Income Thesis

The May 29 Treasury close interrupted the prior session's quality-led duration rally without reversing the week's broader recovery. Front and intermediate maturities retained a small bid, the 10-year finished flat, and the long bond edged cheaper, leaving the curve steeper. The primary interpretation is that declining oil prices removed some immediate inflation compensation and hike risk, yet investors kept a substantial real-rate and term-premium buffer against resilient US activity, an unresolved geopolitical outcome, global central-bank tightening and heavy sovereign duration. This was a repricing of the inflation shock's persistence, not a return to an easing cycle.

Oil-linked relief was the strongest intraday channel. Hopes for a US-Iran agreement initially pulled Treasury yields toward weekly lows, but part of that move faded when the presidential meeting ended without a decision. April trade and inventory releases also resisted a simple slowdown narrative: the goods deficit narrowed as both exports and imports rose, wholesale stockbuilding slowed, and retail inventories exceeded expectations. Month-end index demand supported coupons into the close after an unusually large slate of new notes and bonds entered the benchmark, although that mechanical bid did not prevent the 30-year yield from finishing higher.

Policy asymmetry remained the main counterforce. Kansas City Fed President Jeff Schmid emphasized inflation and the need to preserve the option of moving rates in either direction. Michelle Bowman argued that temporary energy inflation could be looked through while credibility remained intact, and Mary Daly saw no urgency to change the current setting. Lower crude therefore softened the immediate tightening impulse, but the range of Fed views left the front end carrying hike optionality. The modest rally in credit total-return gauges, firmer equities and lower volatility showed that the Treasury bid was not a flight from risk.

Overseas developments reinforced the long-end restraint. Euro-area national inflation readings strengthened the case for an ECB increase, and a slightly weak Japanese 2-year auction showed that near-term BOJ normalization still influenced demand despite cooler Tokyo inflation. Weak Canadian growth supplied a genuine offset, but it was insufficient to dominate the global duration channel. The resulting close moved beyond the prior day's growth-led real-yield relief: inflation compensation eased at 10 years, while real yields and the far end stopped rallying. The market reduced the probability of an enduring oil shock more readily than it reduced the price of policy uncertainty, fiscal supply or long-run capital scarcity.

## UST Rates, Fed Pricing, Real Yields, And Curve Structure

The maturity pattern was a shallow bull move through the belly and a selloff at the far end. The 2-year yield closed at 3.98%, down 1 basis point, and the 5-year at 4.13%, down 2. The 10-year held at 4.45%, while the 30-year rose 1 basis point to 4.99%. The 2s10s curve steepened 1 basis point to 47 basis points and 2s30s steepened 2 to 101. 5-year outperformance pushed the 2s5s10s butterfly 3 basis points lower to -17, whereas the 2s10s30s butterfly remained at -7. The shape concentrated relief in the sector most exposed to a reduced near-term inflation impulse while preserving a long-end premium.

The inflation-linked decomposition sharpened that message. The 10-year real yield rose 1 basis point to 2.07%, and the 10-year breakeven fell 1 basis point to 2.38%, leaving the nominal 10-year unchanged. 5- and 7-year real yields were steady at 1.61% and 1.83%, even as the nominal 5-year rallied. At longer maturities, the 20- and 30-year real yields each rose 1 basis point, to 2.49% and 2.71%. Oil relief was therefore visible in inflation compensation, but investors did not lower the real return demanded from long-duration government debt.

That daily hesitation sat inside a stronger week. 2-, 5-, 10- and 30-year yields ended 15, 14, 11 and 8 basis points below the previous Friday, respectively. The rally had retraced part of the war-driven selloff as benchmark oil fell to a 6-week low on expectations that Middle East supply could normalize. On Friday, 2- through 10-year yields reached weekly lows after President Donald Trump said a meeting on Iran was under way. Prices then gave back part of the gain after reports that he left without a final decision. The 30-year yield touched 4.96% intraday but closed 3 basis points above that low, showing how quickly geopolitical relief met long-end resistance.

The US releases offered no single growth verdict capable of forcing a larger move. April's advance goods deficit narrowed to $82.4 billion from a revised $85.3 billion, against an $86.5 billion consensus. Exports rose $8.5 billion to $219.7 billion, while imports gained $5.6 billion to $302.1 billion. The better net-export arithmetic was accompanied by stronger two-way trade, so it did not describe collapsing domestic demand. Wholesale inventories rose 0.5%, below the 0.8% consensus and well below March's revised 1.5% increase. Retail inventories rose 0.7%, above the 0.4% consensus, including gains of 0.6% outside motor vehicles and 0.9% at vehicle dealers. Stockbuilding supported near-term GDP accounting while leaving final demand ambiguous.

Fed communication preserved optionality around that mixed activity picture. Schmid described inflation as too hot after April PCE prices rose 3.8% from a year earlier, and said officials should retain flexibility to move the policy rate up or down as the data evolve. He also characterized the labor market as balanced, arguing that reduced labor-force growth can coexist with slower hiring and stable unemployment. His framing kept an inflation-led increase within the reaction function even without an overheated employment market.

Bowman supplied the internal counterweight. She favored looking through energy-driven inflation when the shock is temporary, because adding restraint could unnecessarily weaken activity and employment. Persistence was her boundary: a longer conflict, sustained oil prices or broader pass-through into PCE inflation could shift her balance of risks. Daly separately said there was no urgency to adjust rates, and Philadelphia Fed President Anna Paulson saw a series of price shocks without evidence of a structural inflation change. Swaps still contained roughly 15 basis points of tightening by year-end, enough to restrain the 2-year rally even as falling crude reduced the immediate case for action.

Technical demand amplified the coupon bid without changing the policy conclusion. About $324 billion of notes and bonds sold during the month joined a major Treasury index at 4 p.m. New York time, prompting unusually heavy futures volume as passive funds rebalanced. The addition was larger than the roughly $240 billion incorporated at the ends of March and April. Prices rose around the rebalance but remained below their intraday highs. This helps explain why the belly finished firmer despite mixed macro data, and why the close did not amount to an independent judgment that the inflation shock or rate-hike tail had disappeared.

A strategy revision released shortly after the 5 p.m. cutoff underscored the residual duration concern without explaining the completed close. Deutsche Bank raised its projected December peak for the 10-year yield to 4.70% from a prior year-end forecast near the prevailing 4.45%, on the view that the Fed had finished cutting and would remain at a long-run neutral setting. The bank distinguished that hold scenario from market pricing that assigned roughly a 55% chance of a hike by year-end. Both paths leave the obstacle to a sustained rally in place: lower oil can reduce inflation compensation, yet durable real-rate relief still requires weaker activity or a clearer policy turn.

## Credit, Funding, And Primary-Market Absorption

Credit ended with modest positive total returns and no visible rupture in short-maturity funding. LQD gained 0.09%, HYG 0.10% and JNK 0.16%, a restrained advance alongside firmer equities and a slightly steeper Treasury curve. Coupon-equivalent bill yields ranged from 3.68% to 3.79%; daily moves across 4- through 52-week maturities were no larger than 1 basis point. The broad configuration was supportive, though the more informative signal came from new-deal pricing: investors supplied capital readily where structure, scarcity or spread compensated them, and remained sensitive to duration and leverage.

Japanese bank capital provided the clearest completed absorption case. Sumitomo Mitsui Financial Group priced a ¥300 billion dual-tranche Additional Tier 1 transaction and attracted roughly ¥390 billion of orders, led by life insurers and trust banks. Both tranches came 2 basis points tighter than comparable Mitsubishi UFJ AT1 securities sold in April, with call structures after 5.25 and 10.25 years. The securities sit low in the capital structure and can convert to equity if capital ratios breach their trigger, so the book reflected demand for compensated subordination, not generic senior-bank exposure.

The deal also carried a supply and timing message. Japanese AT1 issuance had more than quadrupled from a year earlier to ¥710 billion before including the SMFG sale, as banks refinanced ahead of first-call dates and built capital for corporate lending. Expectations of higher BOJ rates encouraged earlier issuance before reset and benchmark costs increased further. Redemptions created replacement demand, while limited subordinated supply helped the book absorb the transaction. Strong access coexisted with an active effort by issuers to lock financing before the domestic rate regime became less favorable.

Credit Agricole's ¥106.5 billion, 7-tranche Samurai offering showed that the same buyer base still required differentiation. Its 5-year A+ notes priced at 58 basis points over TONA mid-swaps with a 2.454% coupon, double the 28-basis-point spread on lower-rated A notes from Sumitomo Mitsui Banking Corp. in April. Overseas issuers had sold ¥662.7 billion in the Samurai market through May 28, the busiest comparable period in 7 years. Higher yen yields improved absolute income, but investors' reluctance to extend maturity made wider-spread intermediate bonds more attractive than longer duration.

Those 2 Japanese transactions reveal absorption with boundaries. Regulatory-capital scarcity let SMFG tighten against a recent comparable, while Credit Agricole paid a conspicuous cross-issuer premium despite its higher rating. Rising risk-free yields did not close the market; they changed what buyers demanded from coupon, spread, call risk and maturity. The distinction also explains why small gains in US credit funds could coexist with a large global issuance calendar. Funding capacity remained available, but pricing power varied by capital-structure position and the burden of duration.

Weekend reporting added a larger prospective test. A JPMorgan-led bank group was replacing a $15 billion Warner Bros. bridge facility with longer-term debt and expanded the term loan B twice during the week. More than $30 billion of orders met the loan, aided by a BB-area credit profile and scarce leveraged-loan supply. The broader Paramount Skydance financing was tentatively divided into about $30 billion of investment-grade bonds, $12 billion of high-yield notes and $7.5 billion of loans, with the sale timing still unsettled. Paramount's effort to secure investment-grade ratings included a public Ellison-family pledge to inject capital if required to keep leverage aligned with forecasts.

That package had not yet established absorption for the full acquisition financing by the end of the window. Its structure nevertheless exposed the market's next fault line. Floating-rate loan demand was strong enough to enlarge the bridge refinancing, and US junk issuance was running more than 35% above the comparable 2025 period. The high-yield bond portion still required investors to price a major concentration of media leverage, while the investment-grade portion depended on rating treatment and sponsor support. A benign aggregate credit close therefore masked a coming allocation decision across rating buckets and financing instruments.

## Global Duration, Central Banks, And Fiscal Transmission

European inflation supplied the strongest same-day global restraint on US duration. May headline rates accelerated to 2.8% in France, 3.3% in Italy and 3.6% in Spain. Germany eased to 2.7%, yet temporary fuel-tax relief and cheaper food accounted for part of the moderation, while underlying price pressure and firms' pricing intentions remained firmer. Markets maintained expectations for a quarter-point ECB increase and another move later in the year. That configuration lifted the global front-end floor and limited the scope for the US long end to treat lower oil as a complete policy reprieve.

ECB commentary placed credibility ahead of a pre-set sequence. Bank of Italy Governor Fabio Panetta said persistent conflict, fuel-stock depletion and rising expectations justified recalibrating policy, but wages were not showing a spiral and medium-term expectations remained anchored. Bulgarian central-bank chief Dimitar Radev argued that acting late could be more costly if expectations loosened. The growth counterweight was visible in weaker French demand and Panetta's warning that Italian activity could stagnate, while Italy's public debt above 130% of GDP left its curve exposed to higher funding costs. 1 increase could address de-anchoring risk; an extended cycle remained dependent on pass-through and growth.

Japan presented a related tension between cooler measured inflation and firm normalization expectations. Tokyo CPI excluding fresh food slowed to 1.3% year on year, the weakest pace in 4 years, and the measure excluding fresh food and energy rose 1.6%. Water, childcare and gasoline support depressed the headline, while service prices rose 1.1% and food excluding fresh items gained 4.1%. Overnight-index swaps assigned about a 77% probability to a near-term BOJ increase. The yen near 159 per dollar and announced food-price increases kept imported and corporate pass-through risks alive despite the softer headline.

The 2-year JGB auction translated that policy tension into demand. Its bid-to-cover ratio fell to 3.7 from 5.24 at the prior sale, close to the 12-month average of 3.74, and the tail was the widest since December. The sale cleared smoothly, but with little urgency from buyers after yields had declined over the week. Investors were balancing multi-decade-high short yields against the risk of another BOJ increase and reduced bond purchases. For Treasuries, a less dependable Japanese front-end rally and more attractive domestic yields raise the hurdle for cross-border demand and help preserve global term premium.

Canada supplied the clearest developed-market growth offset. Q1 expenditure-based GDP was essentially flat, equivalent to a 0.1% annualized contraction, far below the 1.5% consensus after a revised 1.0% contraction in Q4. Residential investment fell at a 7.9% annualized pace. March output by industry declined 0.1% as a 0.8% contraction in goods production outweighed a 0.1% services gain, although the advance April estimate pointed to a 0.4% rebound. The results favored Canadian duration through the growth channel, yet the early Q2 handoff prevented a one-directional recession conclusion.

Australia pointed in the opposite structural direction. A projected data-center investment pipeline above A$155 billion, equal to 5.6% of annual output, was estimated to support about 400,000 jobs, alongside roughly A$200 billion of clean-energy projects. The capital deepening argument supports a higher neutral rate even if eventual productivity gains improve supply. With the RBA cash rate already at 4.35% after 3 increases during the year, the investment boom added another reason for global real yields to remain above pre-pandemic norms. It was a capital-demand channel, distinct from the day's oil-driven inflation relief.

Developments after the New York close strengthened the global handoff but did not belong to Friday's price formation. China's official manufacturing PMI slipped to 50.0 on Sunday, with new orders at 49.9 despite production at 51.2; the non-manufacturing index rose to only 50.1. That mix softened the global-demand impulse while exposing weak order momentum. BOE policymaker Catherine Mann warned on Saturday that a more shock-prone inflation era had replaced the prior period of stability, and markets expected 25 bp UK increase by year-end. On Sunday, ECB member Alvaro Santos Pereira favored quicker action, while Luis de Guindos described a supply shock that would hit inflation before growth. The later signals deepened the policy-growth conflict awaiting the next session.

## EM Sovereigns, Dollar Liquidity, And Debt-Management Risk

Emerging-market debt participated in the calm close without producing a uniform sovereign signal. EMB rose 0.21%, the strongest daily gain among the broad credit funds, as the dollar softened and both oil benchmarks declined. That combination eased immediate pressure on hard-currency funding and energy import bills. Gold's simultaneous rise and the limited scale of the dollar move advised against reading the session as a wholesale retreat from geopolitical risk. Country sensitivity remained tied to import dependence, currency credibility and the willingness of domestic central banks to absorb the inflation shock.

India became the most concrete sovereign-policy case after the US cutoff. The finance ministry warned on Saturday that elevated crude, a depreciating rupee, rising upstream costs and the risk of a weak monsoon required continued vigilance. Wholesale inflation accelerated to 8.30% in April from 3.88%, above a 5.50% consensus, even though retail inflation remained below the RBI's 4% target. The rupee had traded near a record low around 97 per dollar during the month. For local rates and external financing, the transmission runs from the Strait of Hormuz through the import bill and producer costs into consumer prices, currency defense and a narrower policy cushion for growth.

China's weekend PMI added a different EM constraint. Production remained above the expansion line, yet sub-50 new orders and a lower manufacturing headline pointed to weaker external-demand momentum. Commodity importers could gain from softer oil, while exporters still faced an uncertain orders channel. The combination is more favorable to duration than to credit growth, particularly where fiscal or quasi-sovereign borrowers depend on trade cash flow. It also complicates the positive EMB close: lower US yields and a softer dollar support hard-currency returns, but slower Chinese demand can weaken the fundamental outlook of individual issuers.

Fed Governor Christopher Waller described a longer-run dollar-liquidity mechanism on Sunday. Dollar stablecoin issuers generally hold liquid backing assets such as dollars or Treasury bills, and Waller argued that countries adopting them would import US monetary conditions in a way resembling a fixed exchange rate. The channel is prospective: wider adoption could enlarge demand for short Treasury assets and transmit Fed settings more directly into foreign financial conditions. It does not remove reserve, regulatory or currency mismatch risks for sovereign borrowers; it would extend the reach of US policy even when domestic cycles call for a different stance.

## Cross asset

Risk assets reinforced the view that the Treasury move was inflation relief rather than defensive demand. The S&P 500 rose 0.22% and the Nasdaq 100 gained 0.36%, while the semiconductor index was essentially unchanged after a strong week. The VIX fell 2.67% to 15.32. Equities could absorb a small rally in the front and belly without interpreting the mixed trade and inventory releases as a recession warning. Lower volatility also fit the stable bill curve and modestly positive credit returns, leaving no sign that deleveraging forced the Treasury bid.

Commodities carried the clearest causal signal. WTI fell 1.73% to $87.36 and Brent declined 1.77% to $92.05 as investors anticipated progress toward restoring Middle East supply. Those moves reduced the marginal energy pass-through embedded in short- and intermediate-maturity inflation pricing, yet crude remained high enough for Fed and overseas officials to preserve tightening optionality. The intraday fading of the Treasury rally after the US meeting ended without a decision showed that the geopolitical discount was conditional, not settled.

The dollar index slipped 0.11% to 98.91, modestly easing global dollar conditions and complementing the stronger EM debt gauge. Gold moved the other way, rising 1.36% to $4,560.50 despite firmer equities and lower volatility. Its resilience preserved demand for protection against geopolitical, inflation and policy uncertainty even as oil fell. Taken together, cross assets supported a narrow interpretation: the probability of prolonged energy scarcity declined, allowing risk assets and the belly of the Treasury curve to improve, but hedging demand and the long-end real-yield increase kept the broader inflation and fiscal regime unresolved.

## Forward Market Significance

The US activity and labor sequence begins with the May manufacturing ISM at 10:00 ET on June 1, followed by April JOLTS on June 2, ADP and services ISM on June 3, claims on June 4, and the employment report at 08:30 ET on June 5. The decisive pattern is the combination of new orders, hiring breadth and prices paid. Weaker orders and employment alongside cooling price components, followed by payroll growth below the FHLB New York survey's 95,000 estimate or unemployment above 4.3%, would confirm that the week's lower yields can extend through real-rate relief and would place the 2- to 5-year sector under the greatest repricing pressure. Resilient demand, stable labor turnover and firm prices would challenge the oil-relief thesis, preserve Schmid's tightening optionality and leave the curve vulnerable to higher front-end yields as well as a renewed rise in breakevens.

Euro-area flash HICP at 05:00 ET on June 2 is the cleanest test of whether national energy inflation is broadening before the ECB's June 10-11 decision. Headline and core rates above the 3.3% and 2.5% Wells Fargo forecasts, especially with services or non-energy goods accelerating, would confirm the case for more than a 1-step recalibration and keep upward pressure on European yields and the global term premium embedded in long Treasuries. A concentrated energy rise with stable wages and softer underlying prices would support Panetta's meeting-by-meeting caution. The RBI decision at 00:30 ET on June 5 and BOJ meeting on June 15-16 extend the same test to import-sensitive Asia: currency weakness, broader pass-through or a BOJ increase against the current roughly 77% market probability would reinforce cross-border tightening, whereas stable currencies and contained underlying inflation would challenge the view that overseas policy must keep US duration expensive.

Treasury's scheduled buybacks and the Paramount financing will show whether stable funding can absorb both sovereign and corporate supply. The June 3 liquidity-support operation may accept up to $2.0 billion of 20- to 30-year nominal coupons for June 4 settlement; the June 4 cash-management operation may accept up to $12.5 billion of 1-month to 2-year coupons for June 5 settlement. Orderly offers, settlement and bill-market trading would confirm that the month-end rally was supported by functioning intermediation even as the curve steepened. The corporate counterpart is the still-unscheduled package tentatively spanning investment-grade bonds, high-yield notes and loans. Limited pricing revisions, broad books and preservation of the proposed rating split would confirm that demand can digest leverage when structure and sponsor support are credible. Large concessions, a reduced bond component or heavier dealer exposure would challenge the benign credit close and reopen the question of whether duration and acquisition supply are beginning to compete for the same balance-sheet capacity.

section_summaries.market_read: The close favored intermediate relief while leaving the long-end regime premium intact.
section_summaries.next_check: Whether weaker activity can lower real yields is the key test of rally durability.
section_summaries.summary: Lower oil eased near-term inflation pressure and supported the front and belly, but the May 29 Treasury close steepened as real-rate, policy and supply premia restrained the long end. Month-end demand helped coupons without turning geopolitical uncertainty or resilient activity into a clean easing signal.

section_summaries.market_read: Belly strength reflected inflation relief, not a broad removal of policy or term premium.
section_summaries.next_check: Watch whether softer activity pulls real yields lower rather than only breakevens.
section_summaries.section_title: UST Rates, Fed Pricing, Real Yields, And Curve Structure
section_summaries.summary: The curve rallied modestly through 5 years while the long bond cheapened, concentrating oil relief in breakevens and the belly. Real yields rose at 10 and longer maturities, Fed speakers preserved two-way policy optionality, and month-end index demand could not sustain the intraday highs.

section_summaries.market_read: Funding remained functional, but aggregate calm concealed sharp security-level selectivity.
section_summaries.next_check: The Paramount package must show broad demand without large concessions or dealer buildup.
section_summaries.summary: Credit funds posted modest gains and bills stayed stable, while Japanese AT1 and Samurai deals showed capital remained available at differentiated prices. Scarcity and structure supported demand, but duration, leverage and rating treatment still mattered as the large, unsettled Paramount financing approached.

section_summaries.market_read: Overseas policy and capital demand limited how far US long-duration relief could extend.
section_summaries.next_check: Euro inflation breadth and Asian policy pass-through are the next global duration checks.
section_summaries.section_title: Global Duration, Central Banks, And Fiscal Transmission
section_summaries.summary: Firm European inflation and ECB tightening expectations kept a floor under global yields, while a softer Japanese inflation print did not erase BOJ normalization risk. Weak Canadian growth offered an offset, but Japanese auction caution and Australia's capital-demand outlook preserved pressure on global term premium.

section_summaries.market_read: The broad EM bid reflected easier external conditions, not uniform sovereign improvement.
section_summaries.next_check: Watch whether currency stability survives renewed energy costs or tighter global policy.
section_summaries.section_title: EM Sovereigns, Dollar Liquidity, And Debt-Management Risk
section_summaries.summary: A softer dollar and lower oil supported emerging-market debt at the close, easing immediate funding and import pressure. Gold strength and the modest currency move kept geopolitical caution visible, while sovereign outcomes remained dependent on import exposure, currency credibility and domestic policy room.

section_summaries.market_read: Cross assets confirmed narrow inflation relief rather than a flight-to-quality rally.
section_summaries.next_check: Oil's geopolitical sensitivity must ease further for the Treasury rally to broaden.
section_summaries.summary: Equities firmed and volatility fell as lower oil reduced the perceived persistence of the energy shock, supporting a modest Treasury belly bid without a defensive signal. A softer dollar aided external conditions, but stronger gold and firmer long-end real yields showed that geopolitical and policy hedging remained active.

section_summaries.market_read: The next repricing depends on whether real-rate relief survives data, policy and supply.
section_summaries.next_check: Track all 3 tests for confirmation that the rally can move beyond oil relief.
section_summaries.summary: 3 tests will decide whether the weekly Treasury recovery can extend: US activity and labor data, global inflation and central-bank responses, and sovereign-plus-corporate supply absorption. Cooling demand with softer prices would unlock real-rate relief; persistent inflation or costly issuance would restore front-end and term-premium pressure.

methodology_note: Market levels are shown on an NY-close basis unless otherwise noted.

UST 2s10s curve 47 bp (+1 bp) | UST 2s30s curve 101 bp (+2 bp)
overall_summary.headline: Treasury belly gains on oil relief as real yields hold back the long end
overall_summary.session_narrative: Treasuries finished with shallow gains through the belly, a flat 10-year sector and a slightly cheaper long bond. Oil-linked geopolitical optimism eased the near-term inflation impulse, while month-end index demand supported coupons. The move faded from intraday highs because the geopolitical outcome remained unresolved, activity data were mixed rather than weak, and global policy and supply risks kept long-end real yields firm.
overall_summary.summary: The May 29 close kept a modest bid in front and intermediate Treasuries while the long bond slipped, steepening the curve. Lower oil softened inflation compensation, but resilient activity, two-way Fed optionality and global duration supply preserved real-rate and term-premium resistance.
